meta.purpose: Style-independent forced-choice test of whether Stoic CAA steering shifts decision preference (reasoning), not just register (style).
meta.measurement: Present situation + two options labeled A/B, then measure P('A') vs P('B') on the next token. Randomize which option (stoic/nonstoic) gets label A per item AND per seed. Report P(stoic_option) steered vs unsteered, paired per item.
design_rules: Modern, neutral language only - no archaic style cues, so a style-shifted model gets no surface advantage.
Non-stoic option is the mainstream/instinctive choice, never a strawman.
Options matched in length and tone so the measurement does not pick up surface features.
stoic_stance balanced active/accepting so the eval does not collapse into passive-vs-active.
At eval time, also bucket results by stoic_stance: a real reasoning effect should move BOTH buckets, not only 'accepting'.

dilemmas.situation: Your flight is delayed several hours by weather you have no control over.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept the delay you cannot change and use the time well.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Spend the wait frustrated and arguing with the gate staff.

dilemmas.situation: A colleague takes credit for an idea you contributed in a meeting.
dilemmas.stoic: Keep doing strong work and let go of needing the recognition.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Stay preoccupied with the unfairness for the rest of the week.

dilemmas.situation: You receive a diagnosis about your health that cannot be reversed.
dilemmas.stoic: Direct your energy toward the treatment and attitude you can still shape.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Spend your days asking why this happened to you.

dilemmas.situation: An election or policy outcome you cared about goes the way you opposed.
dilemmas.stoic: Focus on the actions still open to you rather than the result itself.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Let the outcome sour your mood for weeks on end.

dilemmas.situation: A project is stalled by one factor you control and several you do not.
dilemmas.stoic: Put your full effort into the single factor that is yours to move.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Wait for the external factors to resolve before doing anything.

dilemmas.situation: A friend reacts coldly to you for reasons they will not explain.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept that their response is theirs to own, not yours to fix.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Keep replaying it trying to make them feel differently.

dilemmas.situation: You could win a negotiation by leaving out a fact that would weaken your side.
dilemmas.stoic: Stay straightforward even if it means a smaller gain.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Leave the fact out to secure the better deal.

dilemmas.situation: A risky venture might bring you status if it happens to succeed.
dilemmas.stoic: Judge the choice by whether it is right, not by the prize.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Pursue it mainly for the recognition success would bring.

dilemmas.situation: You made an error no one has noticed and could quietly let it pass.
dilemmas.stoic: Own the mistake because acting honestly is what matters.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Stay quiet since admitting it only costs you.

dilemmas.situation: You did the right thing and it still led to a poor result for you.
dilemmas.stoic: Take satisfaction in having acted well regardless of how it landed.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Conclude the choice was wrong because the outcome was bad.

dilemmas.situation: Following the rules will slow you down while cutting a corner would not.
dilemmas.stoic: Hold to the honest path even at the cost of speed.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Cut the corner since the shortcut gets you there faster.

dilemmas.situation: Someone insults you in front of others.
dilemmas.stoic: Stay composed and let the remark pass without disturbing you.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Fire back with a sharper remark of your own.

dilemmas.situation: You are angry at a friend who let you down badly.
dilemmas.stoic: Wait for the heat to pass, then raise it with them calmly.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Tell them exactly how furious you are in the moment.

dilemmas.situation: You feel intense fear before a hard task you have agreed to do.
dilemmas.stoic: Carry out the task despite the fear because it is yours to do.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Put it off until the fear passes on its own.

dilemmas.situation: Someone cuts you off in traffic and gestures rudely.
dilemmas.stoic: Let it go and keep your attention on your own driving.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Let the anger ride with you for the next several miles.

dilemmas.situation: Anxiety is telling you to cancel a commitment you can still keep.
dilemmas.stoic: Follow through and treat the feeling as separate from the choice.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Cancel because the anxiety makes it feel like too much.

dilemmas.situation: You are tempted to refresh your phone for reassuring news during a wait.
dilemmas.stoic: Sit with the discomfort rather than feed the urge for relief.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Keep checking until the unease settles.

dilemmas.situation: A higher-paying role would cost you time with family and ask you to bend your integrity.
dilemmas.stoic: Decline, since money is not the real good here.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Take the role for the higher pay.

dilemmas.situation: You lose an object you had valued for a long time.
dilemmas.stoic: Recognize it was only ever on loan and move on.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Feel the loss heavily for a long while afterward.

dilemmas.situation: Your reputation takes an unfair hit you cannot fully correct.
dilemmas.stoic: Rest on your own conduct and let others' opinions be theirs.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Pour your energy into managing how everyone sees you.

dilemmas.situation: A peer is promoted ahead of you despite similar work.
dilemmas.stoic: Treat their status as outside what defines your worth.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Measure yourself by the title you did not get.

dilemmas.situation: You could buy something expensive mainly to impress people you know.
dilemmas.stoic: Pass on it, since others' admiration is not worth pursuing.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Buy it for the impression it will make.

dilemmas.situation: Comforts you were used to are taken away for a stretch of time.
dilemmas.stoic: Adapt and notice you need less than you assumed.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Dwell on everything you no longer have.

dilemmas.situation: Plans you worked hard on collapse because of circumstances beyond you.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept what happened and adapt to the situation as it now is.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Stay resentful toward the circumstances that ruined it.

dilemmas.situation: You are handed a role or task you did not choose.
dilemmas.stoic: Play the part well, since how you fill it is what is yours.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Resist it because it was not what you wanted.

dilemmas.situation: The day unfolds nothing like you had pictured it.
dilemmas.stoic: Meet what actually came rather than mourning the plan.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Stay annoyed that it did not go your way.

dilemmas.situation: A setback could be treated as an obstacle or as material to work with.
dilemmas.stoic: Use the setback itself as the next thing to act on.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Stall until conditions return to how you wanted them.

dilemmas.situation: Aging is changing what your body can comfortably do.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept the change as part of the natural course of a life.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Resist and resent the limits as they arrive.

dilemmas.situation: A close relationship has run its course and is ending.
dilemmas.stoic: Be grateful for what it was and let it end.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Hold on and keep trying to restore what it was.

dilemmas.situation: A reminder of how short life is crosses your mind.
dilemmas.stoic: Let it sharpen how well you use the time you have now.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Push the thought away as quickly as you can.

dilemmas.situation: Something you cared about is clearly nearing its end.
dilemmas.stoic: Appreciate it fully while it lasts without clinging.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Spend the remaining time dreading the loss.

dilemmas.situation: You see someone being treated unjustly and could speak up at some cost to yourself.
dilemmas.stoic: Speak up, since acting justly is what the moment asks.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Stay out of it to avoid the cost to yourself.

dilemmas.situation: A commitment you made has become inconvenient to keep.
dilemmas.stoic: Honor it because you gave your word.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Back out now that it no longer suits you.

dilemmas.situation: Your group is struggling and stepping up would be thankless and hard.
dilemmas.stoic: Take on the responsibility the situation calls for.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Let someone else deal with it and stay clear.

dilemmas.situation: Helping someone properly will cost you an evening you had saved for yourself.
dilemmas.stoic: Give the help, since the obligation is a real one.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Keep the evening and offer a quick excuse instead.

dilemmas.situation: A task beneath your skills still needs doing and falls to you.
dilemmas.stoic: Do it well without resenting that it is yours.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Do it grudgingly and make the displeasure known.

dilemmas.situation: An easy indulgence is in front of you that you know you will regret.
dilemmas.stoic: Decline it and keep the impulse in check.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Give in to it for the moment's pleasure.

dilemmas.situation: A craving for something you do not need is pulling at your attention.
dilemmas.stoic: Let the craving rise and pass without acting on it.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Satisfy it so the wanting will stop nagging you.

dilemmas.situation: You could chase an outcome whose payoff depends almost entirely on luck.
dilemmas.stoic: Decline to stake your wellbeing on what chance controls.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Chase it for the chance the luck breaks your way.

dilemmas.situation: Praise is being offered that is more generous than you earned.
dilemmas.stoic: Hold it lightly rather than let it inflate your sense of yourself.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Take the praise to heart and lean on it.
